claims.claim: The pipeline can run from a source case to Frank, Karthic, natural model responses, Dasha clusters, Judge scores, Zak state, and model rankings for the current SOF case.
evidence: run_source=cases/MarriageSoF_Anglemire v Policemens Benev Assn of Chicago.pdf
protocol_source=cases/MarriageSoF_Anglemire v Policemens Benev Assn of Chicago.pdf
claims.limitation: Current live evidence is one small Statute-of-Frauds case study, not broad legal-domain validity.

claims.claim: Frank produced a source-grounded legal packet with a neutral question and variations for the current case.
evidence: doctrine=Statute of Frauds — agreements made upon consideration of marriage (other than the mutual promise to marry); effect on oral promise to name spouse as beneficiary / settle property
claims.limitation: Run Frank on held-out source cases before broad external-validity claims.

claims.claim: Karthic produced a fresh dynamic rubric with source-supported rows for the current case.
claims.limitation: Add held-out rubric artifact review and later expert agreement.

claims.claim: Benchmarked response models answered naturally rather than through forced legal headings.
claims.limitation: Live model rosters should use question-only prompting by default.

claims.claim: Dasha clustered the current natural-response batch into coherent legal-reasoning groups.
claims.limitation: Run larger natural batches across more model families and held-out questions.

claims.claim: The saved live run bundle is internally reviewable and its major artifacts match the manifest.
claims.limitation: Regenerate bundle audit after any claim-supporting run or artifact rewrite.

claims.claim: Perturbation validation has been implemented as a metamorphic-test path.
claims.limitation: Run invariant and material perturbation tracks with live model responses.

claims.claim: LLM-as-judge scoring produces row-level scores and model rankings for clustered centroids.
claims.limitation: Quantify live repeat or panel stability before stronger reliability claims.

claims.claim: Zak escalation mechanics exist for low-confidence or unstable cases.
claims.limitation: Calibrate live escalation thresholds with repeated judge evidence.

claims.claim: The 500-response stress test supports scale and bookkeeping mechanics, not natural discovery claims.
claims.limitation: Keep this separated from live discovery evidence.

claims.claim: The next live perturbation run is bounded and preflighted before model spending.
claims.limitation: Resolve credential warnings before claim-supporting paid live runs.

claims.claim: The shareable repo artifacts currently pass secret scanning.
claims.limitation: Secret lint is a pattern scan, not a substitute for institutional secret-scanning tools.
